generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor providing 3D point cloud data for environmental perception with high-resolution spatial sensing capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera offering millimeter-precision depth measurements for spatial mapping and object detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor suite collecting real-time vehicle telemetry including motor, steering, servo actuation states, and overall vehicle dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance ACCRelevantSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor system capturing ACC-specific data including projected driving paths, object tracks, and vehicle state information for adaptive cruise control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ACCRelevantData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR processed via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking to generate structured object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Millimeter-precision depth measurements from Intel RealSense D435i camera for spatial mapping and environmental representation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry including motor, steering, servo actuation states, and overall vehicle dynamics transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateTelemetrySensor
]

instance ACCRelevantData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) data including projected driving paths, object tracks, and vehicle state information for real-time processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ACCRelevantSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Data from motor, steering, and servo actuators transmitted as part of the Actuation Functional Entity for closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateTelemetrySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component converting raw LiDAR points into structured object tracks for visualization and ACC logic processing."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital representation of vehicle dynamics and state for real-time monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ACCLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model handling lead vehicle detection, desired speed calculation, and actuation command transmission for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputationEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks generated from raw LiDAR point clouds after clustering and tracking preprocessing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Formatted vehicle state data with unit conversions and message alignment for consistent integration across components."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ACCProcessedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed ACC data including object tracks, projected paths, and vehicle state information ready for cloud-based computation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ACCRelevantData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputationEnabler
]

instance ActuationCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed desired speed and actuation commands generated by ACC logic for transmission to physical actuators."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ACCRelevantData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component applying preprocessing (clustering, noise filtering) to raw LiDAR point clouds to ensure structured, high-fidelity object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central hub for serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion ensuring seamless integration between heterogeneous data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance CloudComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud/edge computing infrastructure handling computationally intensive tasks (sensor fusion, path planning) for ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ActuationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity converting desired speed commands into motor voltage commands for physical actuation via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network enabling modular and interoperable data exchange between software components (Data Pre-Processing FE, Data Collecting FE, Controlling FE)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance WiFiUDPCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi with UDP transport layer protocol for low-latency, high-throughput inter-entity communication between Physical Twin and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state visualization, data logging, and report generation service for operators and developers."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateVisualizationInsight, ProjectedDrivingPathVisualizationInsight, ObjectTrackDetectionInsight, DataExportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud-based computational augmentation service for resource-constrained onboard systems handling ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetectionInsight, DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight, ActuationCommandTransmissionAction, HardwareAbstractionInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Teleoperation and safety-critical intervention service for ACC enabling remote control and emergency actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCEnableDisableAction, DesiredSpeedAdjustmentAction, EmergencyBrakingAction, SystemStateControlAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Displays real-time vehicle state data (speed, acceleration, sensor inputs) for monitoring and troubleshooting."
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Overlays the vehicle's intended trajectory for path planning validation and monitoring."
]

instance ObjectTrackDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identifies and tracks proximity objects (lead vehicles, obstacles) using sensor data for ACC logic."
]

instance DataExportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables saving and generating reports of vehicle state data for post-analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identifies the preceding vehicle using sensor data for ACC logic execution."
]

instance DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computes optimal speed based on distance to lead vehicle using time-to-collision or adaptive logic."
]

instance ActuationCommandTransmissionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sends computed desired speed back to vehicle for execution as part of closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HardwareAbstractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Leverages cloud/edge computing to handle computationally intensive tasks without overloading onboard ECUs."
]

instance ACCEnableDisableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Toggles ACC functionality remotely via the user interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DesiredSpeedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manually sets or overrides the ACC's computed speed for operator control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issues safety-critical commands (hard braking) to prevent collisions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemStateControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manages ACC parameters (time-headway, acceleration limits) dynamically for runtime control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator controlling motor speed based on voltage commands from the Digital Twin's Actuation Functional Entity."
]

// C1: Entities (Fixed)
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Entity hosting general automotive functionalities including data pre-processing, actuation, and device communication."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Entity providing digital representation of the physical twin including behavior, state, and interactions for simulation and control."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Entity offering human-machine interfaces for monitoring, analysis, and control of the physical twin via the Digital Twin."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment
]

instance ObservableManufacturingElement : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle retrofitted with advanced sensor and control technology for ACC testing."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Decentralized architecture with separate hardware components for Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User Entities."
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity, ObservableManufacturingElement
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the following control flows:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Generated by the **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC logic) to adjust motor, steering, and servo systems via the **Actuation FE**.
   - **User-Controlled Commands**: Inputs from the **User Interface FE** (e.g., control panel signals) translated into physical actions (e.g., steering adjustments, motor actuation).

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Standardized via Data Translation FE**: Commands are serialized and aligned with message types defined in the **ISO 23247 Data Translation FE**, ensuring compatibility with physical actuators.
   - **ROS2 Topics**: Internal control messages are published/subcribed via ROS2 topics for real-time processing.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but implied via the **Data Translation FE**’s role in ensuring message integrity (e.g., unit conversions, serialization) and the **Controlling FE**’s logic validation (e.g., ACC path planning).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Actuation FE**: Provides real-time feedback on motor, steering, and servo states back to the Digital Twin Entity for closed-loop control.
   - **Vehicle State Data**: Continuously looped back to the Digital Twin for validation (e.g., comparing commanded vs. actual actuator positions).

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Not explicitly specified**, but the **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC logic) likely prioritizes safety-critical commands (e.g., emergency braking) over user inputs.

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - Implied via **feedback loops** from the **Actuation FE** and **Vehicle State** data, which validate whether commands were executed as intended (e.g., motor RPM matching commanded values).
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., <100 ms) is mandated by standards but not achievable due to hardware constraints. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services or latency requirements beyond this synchronization threshold, though the **ROS2-based architecture** (via individual ROS2 packages/nodes) implies real-time or near-real-time processing constraints for entity-based functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* in the *Physical Twin Entity*)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its constellation through a **hybrid architecture pattern** combining ISO 23247 RA’s entity-based structure with **ROS2 middleware** for component integration. Key mechanisms include:
  1. **Functional Entity (FE) Mapping**: Each FE (e.g., *Data Storage*, *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) is implemented as a **ROS2 package** containing one or more nodes, enabling modular execution on heterogeneous hardware.
  2. **Entity-Based Deployment**: The system deviates from ISO 23247 by expanding the *Physical Twin Entity* to include **general automotive functionalities** (e.g., sensor/actuator abstraction), unlike the standard’s *OME* (resource-specific). This impacts **ownership and engineering processes** due to cross-entity dependencies.
  3. **Resource Management**: Hardware resources (compute, sensors, actuators) are abstracted within the *Physical Twin Entity*, while the *Digital Twin Entity* centralizes data management and control logic. The *User Entity* isolates user-facing components (*3D Visualization*, *Control Panel*) via ROS2’s topic-based communication.
  4. **Coordination**: Synchronization is enforced via **100 ms clock accuracy**, and inter-entity communication leverages ROS2’s **publisher-subscriber model** for real-time data exchange. The absence of a centralized orchestrator suggests a **peer-to-peer coordination** model, with each entity managing its own lifecycle while adhering to the entity-based RA."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly address **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for the Digital Twin (DT) system. However, the **entity-based architecture** implies that fidelity is indirectly managed through the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)**, which must accurately represent the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**. No mention is made of error detection/correction mechanisms, quality assurance processes, or sources of uncertainty (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations). The standard focuses on **functional decomposition** (via Functional Entities) rather than quantitative validation metrics or compliance frameworks."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is defined as follows:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)**: Used for **intra-entity** software component communication, enabling modular, publish-subscribe-based data exchange (e.g., between **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**, and **Presentation FE**).
   - **Wi-Fi (UDP)**: Employed for **inter-entity** communication (e.g., Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User Entity) with **UDP** at the transport layer for low-latency, high-throughput data transmission.

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical/Modular**: Components are organized into **Functional Entities (FEs)** (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Actuation FE**, **Controlling FE**) communicating via ROS2, with inter-entity links over Wi-Fi.
   - **Centralized Data Translation**: The **Data Translation FE** acts as a bottleneck for all data streams, ensuring serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for ACC and vehicle state monitoring (e.g., Fig. 3a shows real-time 3D visualization updates), implying **low-latency** (<100ms) for control-relevant data.
   - **Bandwidth**: Not quantified, but LiDAR point clouds and high-frequency sensor data (e.g., motor telemetry) suggest **high-bandwidth** requirements for Wi-Fi/UDP links.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed**, but ROS2’s **publish-subscribe model** and UDP’s **connectionless** nature may introduce reliability challenges. Redundancy is implied via **feedback loops** (e.g., actuation confirmation) but not specified.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not in Document**: No mention of encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is characterized by the following:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Implied by the **on-board** nature of sensors (LiDAR, motor/steering/servo actuators) and the **Physical Twin Entity**, suggesting **edge computing** for real-time processing (e.g., **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, **Actuation FE**).
   - **On-Premise/Embedded**: The **User Entity** (e.g., virtual cockpit with 3D visualization) likely runs on **on-premise/embedded hardware** (e.g., vehicle infotainment systems) due to latency-sensitive user interactions.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Computational**:
     - **High-Performance**: Required for **LiDAR point cloud processing** (clustering/tracking) and **real-time ACC logic** (path planning, object detection).
     - **ROS2 Nodes**: Distributed across components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**) with potential **GPU acceleration** for LiDAR data (not explicitly stated but inferred).
   - **Storage**:
     - **Data Storage FE**: Handles raw and processed data (e.g., LiDAR scans, vehicle logs) with **reporting capabilities** (e.g., **Reporting FE** generates structured reports).
     - **Not quantified**, but implied to support **high-volume sensor data** (e.g., continuous LiDAR streams).

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Modular ROS2 Architecture**: Enables **horizontal scaling** by adding ROS2 nodes (e.g., additional **Data Collecting FE** instances for more sensors).
   - **Not explicitly detailed** for storage or computational scaling beyond ROS2’s inherent modularity.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Hybrid Edge-Cloud**: While the document focuses on **edge/on-premise** deployment (e.g., vehicle-integrated components), the **Data Translation FE** and **Reporting FE** could theoretically support **cloud offloading** for non-real-time tasks (e.g., analytics, long-term storage).
   - **Containerized/ROS2-Based**: Likely deployed as **microservices** or **ROS2 packages** for portability.

5. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for **ACC and actuation control**, implying **high availability** (e.g., redundant sensor inputs, fail-safes in **Controlling FE**).
   - **Not explicitly detailed**, but the **feedback loops** (e.g., actuation confirmation) suggest mechanisms to ensure reliability.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference architecture**, which defines:
  - **Entity-Based Architecture**: A structured framework with **four core entities** (OME, Data Collection/Control, Digital Twin, User) and **modular Functional Entities (FEs)** for task-specific implementation.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Compliance with **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** is implied for **data exchange** between entities and physical systems, though not explicitly mandated.
  - **Certification/Compliance**: The standard provides a **framework for certification** but does not specify **third-party validation** or **industry-specific certifications** (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443).
  - **Information Exchange**: Part 4 of ISO 23247 (not detailed here) likely addresses **data formats** and **semantic interoperability**, but the provided context focuses on **architectural compliance** rather than implementation-specific standards."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **safety-critical requirements** as referenced in **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems**, ensuring performance compliance under defined operational conditions. The system integrates **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as specified in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 (Release 15)**, which mandates **low-latency communication** for real-time safety-critical data exchange (e.g., ego vehicle state reporting). While no explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., PKI, TTEthernet) or access control mechanisms are detailed, the **safety-critical nature** of the system implies adherence to **functional safety standards** (e.g., ISO 26262-1:2018) for risk mitigation, though this is not explicitly validated in the provided documents. The system’s design must ensure **fail-safe behavior** under fault conditions, as implied by the **real-time data collection and reporting** requirements for ACC functionality."
]
